title: centaurea × buekkensis
intro: Centaurea × buekkensis is a hybrid plant species, resulting from the cross between the Centaurea cyanus and Centaurea triumfetti plants, commonly found in the Bükkszentmárton region of Hungary. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat digestive issues, such as stomach ulcers and diarrhea, due to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects. The health benefits of Centaurea × buekkensis include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and alleviate symptoms of various gastrointestinal disorders. Herbal preparations of Centaurea × buekkensis can be consumed in the form of teas, infusions, or tinctures, with recommended dosages varying depending on the specific health issue being addressed.
